user_outcome: A protocol maintainer can run the Baton v0.15 vendor or check workflow without false script-reference matches, unsupported schema-expression failures, or any partial write being reported as authoritative success.
rationale: This is the staged bootstrap boundary: it repairs the existing vendor transaction and exact-schema preflight before S02 is allowed to replace the pinned protocol. The vendor implementation remains local, non-interactive, and linear in mapped source bytes; it handles no PII, credentials, UI, or accessibility surface and must never log source payloads. Because exact Baton v0.15.1 has no maintainability waiver, the planning-authority role adapter also reviews only this public repository's committed semantic diff through the configured model and persists the exact report identity before the slice may be verified.
in_scope: Replace incremental destination writes with one transaction that materialises every mapped byte, compiles every mapped JSON schema, and, for upstream write mode, constructs the complete replacement bytes for internal/adopt/baton/VERSION from one captured invocation instant before the first primary-worktree mutation; the VERSION candidate is an ordinary transaction member in the same destination ordering, snapshot, apply, rollback, verification, and recovery boundary as every mapped vendor destination.

Apply atomic per-file replacements with rollback of original bytes, modes, and non-existence if any apply step fails; an incomplete rollback remains explicitly non-authoritative beneath the current worktree's resolved Git administrative directory using the deterministic owner-only record and snapshot contract in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 1, and every later write invocation is recovery-only until exact restoration.

Make check-only mode use the identical materialisation and validation path while prohibiting destination-directory creation, temporary files beneath the repository, pin writes, or other worktree mutation.

Constrain script-reference detection to lexical shell/script tokens so prose such as board.json.shared_touchpoints is accepted while a real scripts/example.sh, example.py, or example.mjs reference still fails closed unless mapped.
Compile the exact v0.15 board-v1 expression with its ECMA-262 negative-lookahead semantics without rewriting normative schema bytes or weakening the forbidden-path cases expressed by that pattern.

Keep work O(total mapped bytes plus mapped file count), preserve source file bytes in diagnostics only as path-safe summaries, and expose deterministic errors naming the failed phase and destination without request bodies, secrets, or source payloads.

Before the implemented handoff, execute the exact Baton v0.15.1 maintainability-review operation through the planning-authority bootstrap adapter defined by planning/normative-clarifications.md section 4: construct the canonical committed semantic scope, fingerprint and prompt diff from start_commit to the stable implementation head; run the untouched tagged prompt with temperature 0; validate and commit the Implementer report against exact llm-check-report-v1; pin its committed blob and review_scope.head in status.json; and let the fresh Verifier run and commit its own authoritative report against that same pinned head before verification may PASS.
out_of_scope: S01 changes transaction machinery only: it does not execute the v0.15.1 vendor operation, advance the committed VERSION pin, replace mapped content, update embedded prompts or schemas, or install local Codex and Claude mirrors; those content, pin, and install changes remain S02-v015-parity-and-installs.
Interpreting v0.15 state, board, spec, report, or lifecycle semantics belongs to S03 through S09.
Adding a third-party schema engine or another runtime dependency is prohibited; any such change would require a separate accepted ADR.
Remote fetch authenticity beyond the existing pinned SHA and digest checks is not redesigned in this slice.

S01 does not add the generalized public maintainability command, automated lifecycle coordinator, report-reuse engine, Coach adjudication, track-composition handling, rollback readiness, or planning-to-current cutover; S06 through S13 retain those product outcomes and S13 revalidates every manually produced bootstrap report before activation.

effort_complexity.rationale: The production change remains confined to thirteen vendor/compiler/command touchpoints, but the exact planning-authority Gate-8 evidence must derive a merge-aware semantic scope, canonical fingerprint and two role-owned blob-pinned reports without relying on the not-yet-built generalized engine.

risks.description: A multi-file apply or separate upstream-pin write could leave a mixture of old and new vendored bytes or a pin that describes content which was not installed.
risks.mitigation: Construct the VERSION replacement before mutation from the same captured invocation instant, make it an ordinary transaction member, fault-inject failure at every apply and rollback index, and prove exact bytes/modes/absence restoration across mapped destinations and VERSION.

risks.description: Narrowing script detection could allow a real executable reference through as prose.
risks.mitigation: Table-test bare, relative, absolute-looking, Markdown-code, link-target, punctuation-adjacent, and board.json.shared_touchpoints cases; unknown real script tokens remain blocking.

risks.description: Schema compatibility handling could silently weaken the v0.15 path restriction.
risks.mitigation: Compile from the untouched upstream bytes and mutation-test every accepted and rejected path class named by the exact upstream expression.

risks.description: A restarted process could trust stale, foreign, traversing, missing, or tampered recovery material and either overwrite an unrelated path or resume ordinary vendoring on a non-authoritative tree.
risks.mitigation: Use the deterministic Git-admin-confined owner-only recovery record in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 1; integrity-check repository identity, every destination/snapshot tuple, original bytes/mode/existence and the complete material set before restoration, and fail recovery-only with no ordinary vendor writes on any mismatch.

risks.description: Calling the manual bootstrap a waiver or creating schema-shaped reports without the exact semantic scope would let unreviewed code become verified and leave S13 unable to distinguish evidence from fabrication.
risks.mitigation: Use the exact tagged v0.15.1 scope, fingerprint, prompt, report-schema, role, phase, cycle, committed-blob and ledger contracts; fail closed on any unsupported history or adapter failure; require a separate fresh Verifier authoritative invocation; and make S13 reproduce and revalidate every bootstrap record before current authority.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the transform scans v0.15 prose containing board.json.shared_touchpoints and fixtures containing real shell, Python, and module-script references THE SYSTEM SHALL accept the prose token and reject every unmapped executable reference with its exact lexical token before any write (N-01, N-02, N-10).

WHEN the validator compiles the exact upstream v0.15 board-v1 bytes containing property-name pattern ^(?!/)(?!.*(?:^|/)\.\.?($|/)).+$ THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve those bytes, accept a, .a, a..b, a//b, a/, and .github/workflows/ci.yml, and reject /a, ., .., a/./b, a/../b, a/., and a/.. exactly as planning/normative-clarifications.md section 1 specifies.

WHEN upstream write mode is requested THE SYSTEM SHALL capture one invocation instant and, before mutation, construct the complete VERSION replacement and every mapped destination byte so VERSION participates in the same ordered snapshot/apply/rollback/recovery transaction; IF preflight fails, apply fails, rollback is incomplete, or a later fresh invocation finds missing, foreign, traversing, symlinked, tampered, or integrity-mismatched recovery material under the exact planning/normative-clarifications.md section 1 contract THEN the public command SHALL exit 2, SHALL perform no ordinary vendor write, and SHALL preserve recovery-only authority until every recorded destination's original bytes, mode, and existence are restored and verified.

WHEN a maintainer invokes the public sworn baton vendor command against the v0.15 checkout THE SYSTEM SHALL bind exit 0 to a valid byte-identical check or a fully successful write, exit 1 only to a valid deterministic non-empty check-mode drift diff, and exit 2 to invalid source, operational, preflight, apply, rollback, or recovery failure; --check SHALL exercise the same transform/schema materialisation path, create or modify no repository path including VERSION or Git-admin recovery material, and print no mapped payload bytes.

WHEN the stable S01 implementation and proof checkpoint are committed under protocol.json authority planning THE SYSTEM SHALL execute the exact Baton v0.15.1 maintainability-review operation over the canonical start_commit-to-implementation_head semantic scope, persist a schema-valid blob-pinned Implementer cycle-0 preflight PASS, pin that review_scope.head as maintainability.implementation_head, and require the fresh Verifier to persist one distinct authoritative PASS for the identical input_fingerprint before the slice enters verified; IF scope construction, model execution, schema validation, blob identity, ledger identity, history freshness, or either role verdict fails THEN verification SHALL fail closed without fabricating or deferring per-slice Gate-8 authority to S13 (N-10).
